title: Implement per-assertion provenance model + validator
context: Every field assertion in the catalog must carry per-assertion provenance (value, source, sourceUrl, license, retrievedDate, assertedBy, confidence) so records are auditable and license-cleared (PLAN section 6, M0). This task implements the provenance data model and a validator that rejects unsourced assertions, depending on the datasheet schema (ewing-pdx-schema-001). Binding cancer guardrails (PLAN section 7): open-access/aggregate/de-identified metadata only; no controlled-access (dbGaP/EGA) or patient-identifiable data; COSMIC/OncoKB reference-only (not ingested); provenance on every assertion; links only, no omics/sequence/image payloads re-hosted; no medical advice. No partner/requestor secured yet, so verifiedNeed is false.
objective: Implement the per-assertion provenance model and a validator that rejects any record containing a field assertion lacking a provenance entry.
acceptanceCriteria: Provenance model implements value, source, sourceUrl, license, retrievedDate, assertedBy, and confidence per assertion.
Validator rejects any record with a field assertion that lacks a corresponding provenance entry.
Validator runs in CI and is reused by downstream ingest/release tasks; reuses packages/schema AJV/draft-07 patterns.
Reviewed by the maintainer; merged with unit tests covering missing-provenance rejection.
output: A merged PR adding the provenance model types + validator (with tests) wired for CI use.
